content.full_text:
BOARD of SUPERVISORS

December 11, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Colleagues:

I am writing at the direction of the Board of Supervisors of the City and County of San Francisco.

At its regular meeting yesterday the Board had before it a proposed ordinance which would ease the limitations on the conversion of rental apartments to condominiums. A copy of that ordinance, found in our file 126-90-1, is enclosed. The only action by the Board yesterday was to vote to continue the matter for one week.

At a previous meeting, the Board of Supervisors had voted to excuse two Supervisors, Hon. Angela Alioto and Hon. Richard D. Hongisto, from voting on this ordinance. They were excused, at their request, on grounds of a potential conflict of interest, after having consulted with the City Attorney's office. I understand that Supervisor Hongisto had, in addition, consulted with your office. Supervisor Alioto may also have done so. These two supervisors did not participate in the debate on either the issue or on the motion to continue, and they did not vote on the motion to continue.

A third Supervisor, Hon. Terence T. Hallinan, had consulted with the City Attorney's office, and perhaps with your office, about a potential conflict. Supervisor Hallinan, while not having requested the Board to excuse him from voting, did not participate in the debate and was not in the room when the vote was taken to continue.

Following the vote to continue, the sponsor of the legislation, the Hon. Willie B. Kennedy, said she did not understand why the three Supervisors could not vote on the legislation. She expressed some concern that the advice from the Fair Political Practices Commission staff might have been significantly influenced by what the City Attorney's office reported to the Commission staff.

Then, on motion by Supervisor Kennedy, the Board voted to direct me to inquire of you why the three persons mentioned could not vote on the ordinance, and to direct me to request a written response before next Monday's 2:00 p.m. Board meeting. I so inquire and so request.

For your information, the telephone numbers (all in area code 415) of the three Supervisors are: Angela Alioto 554-7788; Terence Hallinan 554-7766; Richard Hongisto 554-5664. If you are unable to provide a written response before next Monday's Board meeting, you may wish to telephone Supervisor Kennedy at 554-5734.

Sincerely,

John L. Taylor
Clerk of the Board
